creator: dim@FreeBSD.org
text:
It looks like your machine is running out of memory. The test case compiles fine here on a freshly installed 11.1-RELEASE amd64 VM:

$ /usr/bin/time -l clang -cc1 -triple x86_64-unknown-freebsd11.1 -emit-obj -disable-free -disable-llvm-verifier -discard-value-names -main-file-name node_javascript.cc -mrelocation-model static -mthread-model posix -mdisable-fp-elim -relaxed-aliasing -masm-verbose -mconstructor-aliases -munwind-tables -target-cpu x86-64 -dwarf-column-info -debugger-tuning=gdb -coverage-notes-file /usr/ports/www/node/work/node-v10.0.0/out/Release/obj.target/node_lib/gen/node_javascript.gcno -D NODE_OPENSSL_CERT_STORE -D NODE_ARCH=\"x64\" -D NODE_PLATFORM=\"freebsd\" -D NODE_WANT_INTERNALS=1 -D V8_DEPRECATION_WARNINGS=1 -D NODE_OPENSSL_SYSTEM_CERT_PATH=\"\" -D HAVE_INSPECTOR=1 -D HAVE_DTRACE=1 -D __POSIX__ -D NODE_USE_V8_PLATFORM=1 -D NODE_HAVE_I18N_SUPPORT=1 -D HAVE_OPENSSL=1 -D HTTP_PARSER_STRICT=0 -D NGHTTP2_STATICLIB -O2 -Wall -Wextra -Wno-unused-parameter -std=gnu++1y -fdeprecated-macro -ferror-limit 19 -fmessage-length 131 -pthread -stack-protector 1 -fno-rtti -fobjc-runtime=gnustep -fdiagnostics-show-option -fcolor-diagnostics -vectorize-loops -vectorize-slp -x c++ node_javascript-99f8a1.cpp
        6.05 real         2.95 user         1.25 sys
    411780  maximum resident set size
     52004  average shared memory size
       431  average unshared data size
       255  average unshared stack size
     91297  page reclaims
      2937  page faults
         0  swaps
         4  block input operations
        38  block output operations
      2658  messages sent
         0  messages received
         0  signals received
      2954  voluntary context switches
        74  involuntary context switches

It takes roughly 400 M of memory, but if you are doing parallel compile, you might want to turn down the number of simultanous jobs.  Alternatively, add some RAM or swap space.
